subject: Chemistry
title: The Beirut Explosion of 2020
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Beirut Explosion of 2020.

hook.text: On August 4, 2020, 2,750 tons of ammonium nitrate exploded in Beirut's port. The blast was felt 240 km away in Cyprus.

buildup.text: The ammonium nitrate had been stored unsafely in a warehouse for six years. Officials knew but did nothing.

discovery.text: A fire in a nearby fireworks storage reached the ammonium nitrate. The resulting explosion had the force of 1.1 kilotons of TNT.

twist.text: The blast created a visible shockwave and a mushroom cloud. It destroyed 6,000 buildings and left 300,000 people homeless.

climax.text: The explosion registered as a 3.3-magnitude earthquake. It remains one of the largest non-nuclear explosions ever recorded.

punchline.text: Six years of negligence, one spark. The same fertilizer, the same tragedy, 73 years apart.

quiz.question: Why did the Beirut explosion happen?
options: Improperly stored ammonium nitrate ignited after a fire in nearby fireworks
A military weapons depot was deliberately detonated
An earthquake ruptured underground gas lines
